stories.title: Monaco GP Preview — Ferrari Favored, Leclerc Eyes Fourth Pole
stories.angle: Ferrari's best circuit vs. Antonelli's four-race winning streak. Who breaks whom?

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Monaco is NEXT. 🏎️

June 5-7 | Circuit de Monaco
Ferrari are the favorites — slow corners suit the SF-26
Leclerc could take his 4th Monaco pole

Race: Sun June 7 | 2:00 PM ET | Apple TV

#F1 #MonacoGP #Ferrari

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Monaco GP 2026 — Full Weekend Schedule (ET):

🕐 FP1: Fri June 5 | 12:30 PM
🕑 FP2: Fri June 5 | 4:00 PM
🕒 FP3: Sat June 6 | 11:30 AM
🏁 Qualifying: Sat June 6 | 3:00 PM
🏆 Race: Sun June 7 | 2:00 PM

All on Apple TV. 📺

#F1 #MonacoGP

facebook.long_form:
MONACO IS NEXT — AND IT'S FERRARI'S BEST SHOT TO STOP ANTONELLI.

The Formula 1 Louis Vuitton Grand Prix de Monaco is a week away, and the most glamorous race on the calendar brings a fascinating subplot: the tight street circuit of Monte Carlo may be the one place where Ferrari and Charles Leclerc can level the playing field against Kimi Antonelli's dominant Mercedes.

Here's everything you need to know:

FULL WEEKEND SCHEDULE (all times ET):
Practice 1: Friday, June 5 at 12:30 PM
Practice 2: Friday, June 5 at 4:00 PM
Practice 3: Saturday, June 6 at 11:30 AM
Qualifying: Saturday, June 6 at 3:00 PM
Race: Sunday, June 7 at 2:00 PM
All sessions on Apple TV.

WHY FERRARI ARE FAVORITES: Monaco is defined by slow, technical corners where aerodynamic downforce — rather than straight-line power — wins races. The Ferrari SF-26 has shown exceptional low-speed grip, and the circuit plays exactly to Leclerc's strengths as a qualifying specialist. He's taken multiple poles in Monaco and knows every wall and barrier on this circuit from growing up in the principality.

WEATHER: Friday rain (19°C) could shake up FP1 and FP2 practice. Saturday and Sunday forecast sunny and warm at 27°C — perfect racing conditions for qualifying and the race.

It's Monaco. Anything can happen. But Ferrari are smiling heading in.

Who takes pole? Drop your prediction below! 👇
facebook.image_caption:
THE PRINCIPALITY AWAITS. 🏎️
Monaco GP | June 5-7 | Circuit de Monaco
Ferrari are favorites. Leclerc eyes 4th Monaco pole.
Race: Sun June 7 | 2:00 PM ET | Apple TV #F1 #MonacoGP

article.headline: Monaco GP Preview: Ferrari Favored as Leclerc Eyes Another Pole at Home

stories.title: Hamilton's Happiest Ferrari Day — Canada P2 Builds Monaco Momentum
stories.angle: Can Hamilton finally win Monaco wearing red, on the back of his best Ferrari weekend?

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Lewis Hamilton called Canada P2 his "happiest day at Ferrari so far."

Second podium of 2026. Out-qualified Leclerc. Passed Verstappen late.

Now Monaco — the one Grand Prix Hamilton has NEVER won.

Can he change that in June? 🏆

#F1 #Hamilton #MonacoGP

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Hamilton has won 103+ F1 races.

He has NEVER won in Monaco.

He arrives there in the best form of his Ferrari career after Canada P2.

June 7. 2:00 PM ET. Apple TV.

This is the one he wants. 🇲🇨

#F1 #Hamilton #MonacoGP

facebook.long_form:
LEWIS HAMILTON CALLED IT HIS HAPPIEST DAY AT FERRARI. NOW HE WANTS MONACO.

After a difficult debut season with Ferrari in 2025, Lewis Hamilton has found something special in 2026 — and Canada was the moment everything clicked. He finished second behind Kimi Antonelli, overtaking Max Verstappen in the closing laps, and was visibly emotional on the podium.

"Happiest day at Ferrari so far," he said. "I feel very light right now."

It was his best result for the team: his second podium of 2026, and a race where he out-qualified both Leclerc for Sprint and Race. The seven-time World Champion has earned the feeling.

But now Hamilton has his eyes on something bigger.

Monaco. The one Grand Prix that has eluded him in 103+ victories across his career. Hamilton has finished on the podium in Monaco multiple times but has never stood at the top of that famous harbor backdrop holding the winner's trophy.

That changes next week? Maybe. The SF-26 is well-suited to Monaco's slow corners. Hamilton arrives in Monte Carlo in the form of his Ferrari career. The pressure — if you can call it that at 41 years old on a Saturday qualifier — will be self-imposed and entirely welcome.

Monaco. June 7. 2:00 PM ET. Apple TV.

Can Lewis Hamilton finally win the only race missing from his résumé? Let us know what you think below! 👇
facebook.image_caption:
HAMILTON'S HAPPIEST FERRARI DAY — AND NOW MONACO CALLS. 🏆
Canada P2 | "Happiest day at Ferrari so far"
Monaco: The one win missing from his career
June 7 | 2:00 PM ET | Apple TV #F1 #Hamilton #MonacoGP

article.headline: Hamilton's "Happiest Day" at Ferrari Sets Up Monaco's Final Frontier

stories.title: Antonelli's Five-Peat Target — Can He Win Monaco After Four Straight?
stories.angle: The 19-year-old Italian chasing one of F1's most prestigious wins at the most iconic venue.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Kimi Antonelli has won 4 races from 4 pole positions in 2026.

No driver in modern F1 history has done that.

Monaco would be win No. 5 in a row — at the most prestigious street circuit in the world.

He's 19. 👀

#F1 #Antonelli #MonacoGP

facebook.long_form:
CAN KIMI ANTONELLI WIN MONACO AND MAKE IT FIVE STRAIGHT?

In four Grands Prix in 2026, Kimi Antonelli has started from pole position four times and won four times. This has never been done before in the modern Formula 1 era. He leads the championship by 43 points. He is 19 years old.

Now comes Monaco.

The most prestigious street circuit in the world, the race every driver wants to win, the venue that has produced legends and heartbreak in equal measure. Neither Antonelli nor his Mercedes teammate George Russell has ever won in Monte Carlo.

Ferrari are slight favorites for a reason — their car's low-speed characteristics are well-suited to the 19 corners that make up the Circuit de Monaco. But momentum has been with Antonelli all season, and his ability to qualify on the front row and manage races with composure beyond his years has been remarkable.

If Antonelli wins Monaco, he will have done something that has never been achieved in the sport's history. Five consecutive wins from five consecutive poles.

Can a teenager from Italy end Monaco's resistance to Mercedes? We're about to find out.
facebook.image_caption:
4 RACES. 4 WINS. 4 POLES. 🏆
Can Kimi Antonelli make it FIVE STRAIGHT at Monaco?
June 7 | Circuit de Monaco | Apple TV #F1 #Antonelli #MonacoGP

article.headline: Antonelli's Five-Peat: Can the Championship Leader Win Monaco and Make History?

stories.title: Russell's Recovery Mission — DNF Canada, 43 Behind, Monaco Must-Deliver
stories.angle: Russell's nightmare Canada DNF makes Monaco a near must-win.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
George Russell was LEADING the Canadian GP when his car stopped on Lap 30.

He's now 43 points behind Antonelli.

Monaco is next. One of the few circuits where Russell can overperform.

The championship is still alive. Just.

#F1 #Russell #MonacoGP

facebook.long_form:
GEORGE RUSSELL WAS LEADING THE RACE WHEN HIS CAR DIED. MONACO IS HIS RESET.

The Canadian Grand Prix could not have been more cruel for George Russell. He held the lead for most of the race, trading positions with teammate Kimi Antonelli in what was shaping up to be one of the great intra-team battles in recent memory. Then, on Lap 30, his car stopped. Technical failure. Race over.

The championship gap that had been 20 points grew to 43 in an afternoon.

Russell now faces a stark reality: he needs to close ground on Antonelli before the summer, and Monaco — a circuit where mistakes are magnified, where patience is rewarded, and where the fastest qualifier often wins — could be his best chance.

Russell has been strong at street circuits throughout his career, and Mercedes drivers have generally performed well in the principality. But with Ferrari and Leclerc targeting their home advantage, he'll need to be at his absolute best.

43 points is a manageable gap with race weekends still ahead. But with Antonelli's form, "manageable" feels like an optimistic word right now.

Monaco. One week. George Russell needs a result.
facebook.image_caption:
43 POINTS DOWN. ONE WEEK TO RESPOND. 🇲🇨
Russell was LEADING Canada when his car stopped on Lap 30.
Monaco next | June 7 | 2:00 PM ET | Apple TV #F1 #Russell #MonacoGP

article.headline: Russell's Montreal Nightmare: Leading the Race, Then Nothing — Monaco Is His Reset

stories.title: Verstappen Targets Monaco Redemption — First 2026 Podium Fuels Hope
stories.angle: Verstappen the Monaco specialist — can he turn the circuit magic into a 2026 win?

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Max Verstappen finally got his first 2026 podium in Canada (P3).

He's also won Monaco in 2021 and 2023.

Red Bull may not have the fastest car — but Verstappen's Monaco magic is real.

Watch out on June 7. 🏁

#F1 #Verstappen #MonacoGP

facebook.long_form:
VERSTAPPEN FINALLY GOT HIS FIRST 2026 PODIUM. NOW HE TARGETS MONACO.

It took five races, but Max Verstappen finally stood on the podium in 2026 — finishing third in Canada in what Red Bull Racing team principal Laurent Mekies called a "truly historic" moment for their power unit.

For a four-time world champion, ninth in the standings is not where he wants to be. The new 2026 regulations have not favored Red Bull. Verstappen has been patient. And his first podium of the season — however hard-earned — signals that the car may be moving in the right direction.

Next up: Monaco.

This is a circuit that Verstappen knows intimately. He won there in 2021 and again in 2023, both times showcasing the pure car-control skill that separates him from nearly everyone else in the field. Monaco places a premium on driver precision over pure car speed, which means Verstappen can make up for any Red Bull deficit with sheer talent.

His contract also includes an exit clause tied to his championship position at the summer break. With one podium banked and Monaco on the horizon, the pressure is building in both directions — to perform, and to stay.

Can Verstappen use Monaco to announce that Red Bull is back? June 7 is the date. Mark it down.
facebook.image_caption:
FIRST 2026 PODIUM IN CANADA. NOW MONACO CALLS. 🏁
Verstappen won Monaco in 2021 and 2023.
Can the magic work in 2026? June 7 | Apple TV #F1 #Verstappen #MonacoGP

article.headline: Verstappen's Monaco Magic: First 2026 Podium Opens the Door in the Principality
